summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes XOR-obfuscated binary from GitHub, then self-erases
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook (install-hook.js) executes a base64-encoded UTF-16LE PowerShell payload via `powershell -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Enc`. The payload downloads `launcher.bin` from a personal GitHub repository (github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note, releases/download/v2/launcher.bin), XOR-decrypts it with key 0x42, writes the result to `%TEMP%\tmp.exe`, executes it hidden, and deletes it. After launching the dropper, a second hidden PowerShell process walks up to the consumer project root, removes the `mailconfirmer` entry from `package.json` and `package-lock.json`, recursively deletes its own `node_modules/mailconfirmer` directory (using `MoveFileEx` to schedule reboot-delete if files are locked), and stops a watchdog process at `%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Caches\dllhost.exe` — implying persistence has already been established elsewhere on the host. The package's advertised functionality is a cover story: index.js only exports two stub functions that `console.log` placeholder messages, with no real email logic. The dropped binary's publisher (a personal GitHub account) is unrelated to the package author, the binary is unpinned and obfuscated, and the post-install anti-forensics (lockfile/package.json scrubbing, self-delete, watchdog reference) confirm malicious intent against the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
